steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage sets up a scholarly prediction (liberalization helps companies raise money) and Henry's empirical finding (11 liberalizing countries saw significant investment increases). The blank must describe what this finding reveals about that prediction.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "companies in Bangladesh experienced a greater increase in investment following liberalization than did companies in Malaysia.": No such detail appears in the passage. ✗
  • "economists who held the standard view of liberalization failed to anticipate some serious negative effects of liberalization.": The opposite — Henry's data matched the prediction. ✗
  • "companies did not benefit from liberalization until at least three years after liberalization occurred.": The passage says investment increased during the three years following liberalization; it does not say companies waited at least three years to benefit. ✗
  • "empirical evidence was consistent with the scholarly consensus about the consequences of liberalization.": Henry found investment increases following liberalization, so the actual outcome matched what scholars predicted. ✓
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
